title: Fix: run_once_remove-claude-code-cask.sh breaks Windows CI (missing chezmoiignore entry)
description: The migration script added in PR #124 (dotfiles-pcg) has a runtime 'case uname Darwin' guard that would no-op on Linux/Windows if the script ran. But on Windows, chezmoi can't even exec the .sh file — Windows rejects POSIX-shebang scripts at the CreateProcess level with 'not a valid Win32 application'. The runtime check never gets a chance to fire. Root cause: the script is missing from .chezmoiignore, so it gets deployed on all OSes and Windows tries to run it, crashing the install-test CI job on every push to main since PR #124.
design: Add 'run_once_remove-claude-code-cask.sh' and 'remove-claude-code-cask.sh' to BOTH the 'Only apply Unix files on Unix' block (line 23-42) AND the 'OS-specific files / non-darwin' block (line 51-59) in home/.chezmoiignore. Matches the existing pattern for run_once_after_install-brewfile.sh which is macOS-only for the same brew-dependent reason. Keep the runtime Darwin guard in the script body as belt-and-suspenders in case the .chezmoiignore pattern is ever misconfigured.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Windows test-install CI on main passes after merge. (2) macOS test-install still runs the script. (3) Linux test-install either skips the script (via chezmoiignore) or runs and immediately exits 0 via the Darwin guard. (3) chezmoi dry-run on macOS shows the script is deployed. (4) chezmoi dry-run on Windows shows the script is ignored. (5) shellcheck and markdownlint still pass on other files.
close_reason: Fix delivered in PR pmgledhill102/dotfiles#127. PR-level CI green on lint checks. Note: PR-stub test-install does not actually verify the Windows fix — real verification happens on push-to-main CI after merge.

title: CI: strip pre-installed tools for realistic testing
description: macOS/Ubuntu CI runners come with tools pre-installed (Homebrew, git, zsh) that mask bootstrap failures. Strip these after checkout to simulate a fresher state.

title: Fix missing git dependency on Ubuntu/Linux
description: run_once_install-packages.sh uses git clone for OMZ plugins and nanorc, but git is not in the apt package list. On a fresh Ubuntu, git may not be present.

title: Bootstrap prerequisites in install.sh for fresh machines
description: On a fresh macOS, git is just an Xcode CLT stub that triggers a dialog. install.sh needs to install Xcode CLT and Homebrew before chezmoi runs. On fresh Linux, git/curl may be missing too.

title: Fix Starship language version check timeouts
description: On work machine, Python and Go version checks in Starship regularly exceed the 500ms default timeout, cluttering shell output with warnings. Fix: set command_timeout = 2000 (2 seconds) in the Starship config. This applies across all machine types. The Starship config is likely at home/dot_config/starship.toml or similar chezmoi-managed path.

title: Skip font validation in CI
description: font-jetbrains-mono-nerd-font cask silently fails on GitHub Actions runners. The validation script then fails the entire CI run. Fix: detect CI=true and downgrade the font check to a warning.

title: CLI menu-driven machine type selection for chezmoi init
description: Add an interactive CLI menu during chezmoi init that asks what 'type' of machine is being provisioned (e.g. personal, work, server/minimal). On a work machine, all config should be applied but application installation should be selective — skip most Brewfile apps and VS Code extensions, but still install 'essential' software. This requires: (1) a chezmoi template prompt or init script that sets a machine_type data field, (2) conditional logic in Brewfile.tmpl to split packages into essential vs full, (3) conditional VS Code extension installation, (4) persisting the choice in .chezmoi.toml so subsequent applies respect it.

title: Activate age-encrypted secrets management
description: The entire secrets pipeline is designed and documented but not turned on. .chezmoi.toml has no [age] section, and dot_secrets.tmpl is a placeholder. Until this is live, API keys have to be manually managed on each machine, defeating the purpose of chezmoi. Steps: generate age key, add [age] to .chezmoi.toml, chezmoi add --encrypt ~/.secrets, update .chezmoiignore for conditional handling, document key storage in Bitwarden.

title: Wire up .zshrc tool integrations (fzf, nvm, jenv, EDITOR)
description: fzf, nvm, and jenv are all installed via Homebrew but never initialized in .zshrc. Also remove debug echo statements and set $EDITOR. This is the highest-bang-for-buck fix — it's the daily shell experience.

title: Add fast-CI test-install variant that runs chezmoi apply but skips package installs
description: Replace the test-install-pr stub (which currently just echoes 'Skipping full install test on PR') with a real fast-CI variant that runs chezmoi init --apply on all 3 OSes but skips the slow brew/apt/winget package installs. This catches shebang-exec issues, chezmoi template bugs, and chezmoiignore misconfigurations pre-merge rather than post-merge — exactly the class of bug that broke Windows CI in PR #124 (dotfiles-zg8).
design: Env var DOTFILES_SKIP_INSTALL=1 controls gating end-to-end. Template-time gates in the 5 expensive .tmpl scripts (before-install, after-install-brewfile, install-packages-windows — winget loop only, install-ubuntu-packages, after-install-cship) render a no-op stub when the env var is set. Both validation scripts get a validate_test_pkg / Test-Validation-Pkg wrapper that skips package-dependent binary checks in fast mode while keeping file-existence checks as hard failures. New CI job replaces test-install-pr stub with same name 'Test Install on ${{ matrix.os }}' (preserves branch-protection required checks), drops strip step + disk-space telemetry + zsh-startup benchmark, sets DOTFILES_SKIP_INSTALL=1, runs in ~3-5 min per OS.
acceptance_criteria: (1) New fast CI job runs on every PR and completes in <5 min per OS. (2) Template gates produce no-op stubs when env var set, full script when unset — verified via chezmoi execute-template in both modes. (3) Both validation scripts skip binary-dependent checks when env var set, still hard-fail on missing file-existence checks. (4) The post-merge full test-install job on main is unchanged and still runs the full package install path. (5) shellcheck clean on all changed .sh / .sh.tmpl files. (6) Branch protection check names unchanged ('Test Install on ubuntu-latest', 'macos-latest', 'windows-latest'). (7) Deliberate-break test: a broken shebang script causes fast CI to fail.
notes: Plan file at /Users/paul/.claude/plans/encapsulated-wondering-haven.md. Files touched: .github/workflows/ci.yml, home/run_once_before_install-packages.sh.tmpl, home/run_once_after_install-brewfile.sh.tmpl, home/run_once_install-packages-windows.ps1.tmpl, home/run_onchange_install-ubuntu-packages.sh.tmpl, home/run_onchange_after_install-cship.sh.tmpl, scripts/validate-installation.sh, scripts/validate-installation-windows.ps1.
close_reason: Delivered in PR pmgledhill102/dotfiles#128. All 7 CI checks green on first real exercise. Fast CI runtimes: ubuntu 13s, macOS 14s, Windows 27s (plan estimate was 3-5 min each — significantly better than expected). Validation summaries: ubuntu 15 passed / 24 skipped / 0 failed; Windows 19 passed / 17 skipped / 0 failed. All three OSes actually ran chezmoi init --apply and would have caught the PR #124 shebang bug pre-merge. Awaiting human review and merge.

title: Add CLI ergonomics to note/notes functions (help, --edit, stderr)
description: Uplift the note/notes shell functions to follow standard macOS/Ubuntu CLI conventions without becoming full CLIs. Add --edit as a long-form synonym for -e; add -h/--help/-? flags to both note and notes; the help output should describe both commands since they are a single feature surface. Also ensure error messages go to stderr, exit codes are non-zero on error, and '--' end-of-options separator works for notes that start with a dash.
design: Add a shared help-text function inside note.zsh that is invoked by both note and notes. note() dispatches help on -h|--help|-?, dispatches edit mode on -e|--edit, and uses '--' to mark end of options (lets users write notes that start with a dash). notes() adds the same help flags and delegates to the same help-text function. Exit 0 on success, 1 on usage error. Error messages to stderr via 'print -u2' or '>&2'.
acceptance_criteria: (1) 'note -h', 'note --help', 'note -?' all print the same help text covering both note and notes. (2) 'notes -h' etc. same. (3) 'note --edit' opens $EDITOR, equivalent to 'note -e'. (4) 'note -- --starts-with-dash' appends that literal text as a note, not parsed as a flag. (5) Error messages (unknown flag, usage errors) go to stderr and exit non-zero. (6) shellcheck clean. (7) Functional test driver exercises every new flag and the '--' separator.
close_reason: Delivered as PRs #125 (note/notes CLI ergonomics) and #126 (dotfuncs listing). Both all 7 CI checks green. Independent branches, both merge cleanly from main. Awaiting human review and merge per project policy.

title: Switch claude-code brew cask to @latest channel with migration script
description: The default 'claude-code' brew cask intentionally tracks ~1 week behind upstream (currently v2.1.91 vs v2.1.101 latest). The 'claude-code@latest' cask tracks the upstream release channel directly. Switch the Brewfile to use the @latest variant so dotfiles-managed machines stay current. Required because Claude Code is iterating fast and the lag is starting to bite (e.g. the Grep tool 'rg' posix_spawn bug may already be fixed in versions newer than 2.1.91).
design: Edit home/Brewfile.tmpl line 62 from 'cask "claude-code"' to 'cask "claude-code@latest"'. Add a one-time chezmoi run_once_ migration script at home/run_once_remove-claude-code-cask.sh that detects and uninstalls the legacy 'claude-code' cask on existing machines before brew bundle install runs the new @latest cask (both casks install /opt/homebrew/bin/claude — they conflict). Script is idempotent: no-op on Linux, fresh installs, or already-migrated machines.
acceptance_criteria: (1) Brewfile.tmpl renders with claude-code@latest in chezmoi dry-run. (2) shellcheck clean on the new migration script. (3) Migration script is no-op when claude-code cask is not installed. (4) Migration script successfully uninstalls claude-code cask when present (manual test on user's machine). (5) chezmoi run_once_ prefix ensures the script runs exactly once per machine. (6) CI green: shellcheck, markdown-lint, action-lint, test-install matrix.
notes: Separate PR from the note function PR #123 per single-concern rule. User decided not to wait for v2.1.101 fix-confirmation on the rg bug — wants to be on latest regardless given how fast Claude Code is iterating.
close_reason: Delivered in PR pmgledhill102/dotfiles#124. CI green (shellcheck, markdown-lint, action-lint, test-install matrix). Awaiting human review and merge per project policy. Manual local test pending — user will run brew uninstall/install dance to switch their machine to claude-code@latest.

title: Add note/n shell function for quick project-scoped notes
description:
Add a lightweight `note` zsh function (with `n` alias) for jotting project-scoped notes from the shell. Notes append to `~/notes/<project>.md` where <project> is the git repo basename (or cwd basename when not in a repo). Append-only; edits via `note -e` opening $EDITOR; viewing via `note` (no args) prints current project file. A plural `notes` command lists/greps across all note files. Research confirmed no existing tool (jrnl, nb, dnote, nap, zsh plugins) cleanly satisfies the constraints — a small zsh function is the right call.

This work also includes a small refactor: extract the existing functions in `home/dot_config/zsh/aliases.zsh` (dotup, dotstatus, dotbrew, dotclaude, _dotclaude_bw_get) into individual files under `home/dot_config/zsh/functions/`, and add a loader to `dot_zshrc.tmpl` that sources every `*.zsh` file in that directory. `aliases.zsh` is left holding only the actual aliases (bat, podman). This makes each command's definition explicit and obvious.
design: Storage: ~/notes/<git-basename>.md, fall back to cwd basename outside repos. Format: markdown bullets prefixed by short timestamp `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM`. View: `note` (no args) prints file; `note -e` opens $EDITOR. Plural `notes` for cross-file ls/grep. `n` aliased to `note`. File layout: home/dot_config/zsh/functions/{dotup,dotstatus,dotbrew,dotclaude,note}.zsh; loader in dot_zshrc.tmpl uses a for-loop with -f guard for shellcheck friendliness. dotup() needs its reload line updated to re-source the functions/ dir as well as aliases.zsh.
acceptance_criteria: `note 'foo'` appends a timestamped bullet to ~/notes/<project>.md; `note` with no args prints the file; `note -e` opens it in $EDITOR; `n 'foo'` works as alias; `notes` lists files, `notes grep 'pat'` searches; existing dot* functions still work after refactor; shellcheck and markdownlint pass; chezmoi dry-run apply succeeds in a clean HOME.
close_reason: Delivered in pmgledhill102/dotfiles#123 — CI green (shellcheck, markdown-lint, action-lint, test-install matrix). Awaiting human review before merge per project policy.

title: Replace Docker with Podman across all environments
description: Switch container tooling from Docker to Podman. Remove Docker Desktop from Windows winget, add podman to work tier brew, add podman to Linux apt, add docker=podman alias, update Claude Code permissions and MCP config.
close_reason: Implemented: Podman replaces Docker across macOS (brew), Linux (apt), Windows (removed Docker Desktop). Shell aliases, Claude Code permissions, MCP setup all updated.

title: Review and curate VS Code extensions
description: Audit the current VS Code extension lists and propose additions. Two sources exist: (1) _todo_resources/vscode-exts.md — 24 extensions (gitlens, githistory, PowerShell, markdown-all-in-one, python, eslint, prettier, remote-wsl, yaml, xml, Go, remote-ssh, markdownlint, github-pr, drawio, markmap, slidev, csharp, jupyter, shellcheck, rust-analyzer). (2) gamer-build-files install-apps.cmd — 40+ extensions (superset of above, plus Docker, Terraform, CloudCode, Azure, AWS, Copilot, LiveShare, etc.). Tasks: (a) Merge both lists, deduplicate. (b) Categorise by purpose (core, language-specific, cloud/infra, productivity). (c) Map to machine types (personal gets all, work gets subset, minimal gets none). (d) Research and suggest additional extensions based on the user's stack: chezmoi/dotfiles, zsh, Go, Python, Rust, .NET, Node/TS, Terraform, AWS/Azure/GCP, Docker, GitHub, markdown/docs. (e) Flag any deprecated or superseded extensions. (f) Propose the canonical list for the chezmoi-managed install script.
close_reason: Completed in Phase 11 PR #44 — extensions curated and auto-install implemented

title: Migrate Windows app installs from gamer-build-files repo
description: The private repo desertfoxclan/gamer-build-files has rebuild-src/install-apps.cmd with comprehensive winget installs for Windows. This should be migrated into the dotfiles chezmoi structure. Current content in install-apps.cmd: Core apps (NVIDIA, VC++ redist), Dev tools (VS Code, IntelliJ, Git, Docker, Hugo, etc.), Languages (Go, Node, Java, Python, Anaconda, Rust, .NET 9, Ruby), Cloud SDKs (AWS, Azure, GCP), VS Code extensions (40+), System utilities (CrystalDiskInfo, HWiNFO, etc.), Office (via office-config.xml), General tools (7-Zip, VLC, Chrome, Discord, Bitwarden), Games (Steam, Epic, GOG, etc.), Windows Store apps (Affinity suite), PowerShell modules, Registry tweaks. Migration approach: (1) Add winget package IDs to .chezmoi.toml.tmpl under a windows/personal machine type. (2) Create run_once_install-windows-apps.ps1.tmpl using chezmoi templates. (3) Separate into categories (dev, gaming, productivity) that can be toggled per machine type. (4) Handle VS Code extensions separately (merge with existing extension management). (5) Migrate registry tweaks to run_once_setup-windows-environment.ps1.tmpl.
close_reason: Migrated ~60 winget packages, 7 VS Code extensions, and game launcher pinning into chezmoi dotfiles. PR #46 created.

title: Investigate and automate maintenance tasks
description: Review what maintenance the dotfiles repo needs and how much is already automated. Current automation: (1) package-updates.yml — weekly check for Homebrew outdated + Starship releases, creates GitHub issues. (2) ghostty-terminfo.yml — weekly regeneration of xterm-ghostty terminfo. (3) dependabot.yml — weekly GitHub Actions version bumps. (4) pre-commit — markdownlint-cli2. Gaps to investigate: (a) No automated chezmoi version tracking. (b) No Oh My Zsh plugin update detection. (c) No shell startup time regression monitoring. (d) No automated apt/winget package freshness checks (only Homebrew). (e) docs/MAINTENANCE.md describes monthly/quarterly/annual tasks but none are automated or tracked. (f) No Brewfile.lock or pinned versions for reproducibility. Propose which gaps are worth filling and how (GitHub Actions, chezmoi hooks, or manual checklist).
close_reason: Added chezmoi and OMZ plugin checks to weekly workflow, trimmed MAINTENANCE.md. PR #51.

title: Review docs and populate root README.md
description: The docs/ directory has 5 files totalling ~2900 lines: BACKUP_RECOVERY.md (577), MAINTENANCE.md (521), MIGRATION.md (649), TESTING.md (332), TROUBLESHOOTING.md (830). For a personal dotfiles repo, much of this is over-engineered. Propose a trimmed documentation set. Candidates for trimming/merging: MIGRATION.md (one-time use, could become a section in README), BACKUP_RECOVERY.md (mostly theoretical — keep age key backup essentials, drop the rest). The root README.md is currently empty and should become the primary entry point covering: what this repo is, quickstart install, what's included (tools/platforms), machine types, and links to detailed docs. Also review CONTRIBUTING.md, AGENTS.md, GEMINI.md at root level for relevance.
close_reason: README populated, docs trimmed (5→2 files), AGENTS.md trimmed (129→25 lines), CI references updated across CONTRIBUTING.md and TESTING.md

title: Consolidate specs into single requirements document
description: The specs/ directory has 7 files (spec.md, plan.md, research.md, tasks.md, quickstart.md, checklists/requirements.md, checklists/testing.md) from an earlier spec-driven workflow that isn't being followed. Consolidate the valuable content into a single REQUIREMENTS.md (or similar) with sections: Constraints, Principles, Requirements, Key Decisions, Open Items. Also clean up scaffolding directories: .specify/ (templates, scripts, memory/constitution.md), .gemini/ (commands), _todo_resources/ (3 files: vscode-exts.md, fix-wsl-path.sh, win-term-profile.ps1 — migrate useful content first). The quickstart.md content should move to the root README.md (see sister issue).
close_reason: Consolidated 7 specs files into specs/REQUIREMENTS.md, deleted .specify/ and .gemini/ scaffolding

title: T068: Python/uv support across all platforms
T065: VS Code extension auto-install
T066: Windows Terminal dev directory
T067: Delete _todo_resources/
T063: PowerShell on Ubuntu
T062: WSL PATH fix via wsl.conf
Add bat/delta shell aliases and CLI UX defaults
POC: chezmoi + Bitwarden secrets
Add dotfiles shell aliases and update README
description: Add a dedicated aliases file to zsh config, create dotup alias (chezmoi update -v) and dotstatus function (shows machine type, last applied, pending changes). Update README update section to recommend chezmoi update as primary method.

title: Add bat/delta shell aliases and consistent CLI UX
description: bat and delta are installed but the shell doesn't default to them. Add alias cat=bat, set BAT_THEME, export EDITOR='code --wait', and tie the whole tool chain together into a cohesive CLI experience.

title: Finish Phase 11 — clean up _todo_resources/
description: 8 open tasks from the original spec (T061-T068): VS Code extension auto-install (T065), WSL PATH fix (T062), Windows Terminal profile migration (T066), Python/uv support (T068), and others. Once done, _todo_resources/ gets deleted and the spec is fully complete.

title: Configure Ghostty terminal (font, theme, keybindings)
description: home/dot_config/ghostty/config is essentially empty — just boilerplate comments. Ghostty is in the Brewfile cask, JetBrains Mono NF is installed, and the terminfo CI workflow exists, but the terminal itself has no font/theme/keybinding config. One file, big quality-of-life win.
close_reason: Stripped to clean placeholder — Ghostty defaults are fine (PR #41)

title: Add zoxide with minimal zsh integration
description: Install zoxide via Brewfile and wire up minimal zsh integration (z/zi commands only, no cd-replacement). Remove conflicting OMZ z plugin from plugins list since both define a z command.

title: Harden dotup against rustup update hangs
description:
On work machines with EDR/antivirus, 'rustup update' can hang for hours during the 'cleaning up downloads & tmp directories' phase because it walks ~/.rustup/tmp and ~/.rustup/downloads file-by-file and each deletion gets scanned. This blocks the rest of dotup from running.

Options to consider:
1. Wrap the rustup update step with a timeout (e.g. 5 min) so a stall doesn't block subsequent steps
2. Add a DOTUP_SKIP_RUST=1 env var escape hatch
3. Pre-clean ~/.rustup/tmp and ~/.rustup/downloads with rm -rf before invoking rustup update (much faster than rustup's per-file cleanup)
4. Move rust update behind an explicit flag/opt-in

See home/dot_config/zsh/functions/dotup.zsh:33-36 for the current unconditional invocation.

title: Add support for optional ~/.zshrc.local for machine-local overrides
description: Allow users to add work-specific or machine-specific zsh settings in ~/.zshrc.local that persist across dotfiles updates (dotup). Sourced at the end of .zshrc so it can override anything above.

title: Replace tfenv with tenv in Brewfile
description: Switch Terraform version manager from tfenv to tenv in both personal and work Brewfile blocks. tenv is a Go-based superset that also handles OpenTofu, Terragrunt, and Atmos, with faster shell startup and reads the same .terraform-version files.

title: Add GIMP and Inkscape casks to Brewfile
description: Install GIMP and Inkscape via Homebrew casks on dotfiles-managed machines — user wants them available without manual install on fresh provisioning. Two one-line additions to home/Brewfile.tmpl: 'cask "gimp"' and 'cask "inkscape"'. Brewfile is alphabetically sorted, so: gimp goes between 'ghostty' and 'google-chrome'; inkscape goes before 'insomnia' (ink < ins).
design: Two one-line additions to home/Brewfile.tmpl. Open questions when picking this up: (1) should they be gated on machine_type==personal like some other casks, or deployed on all machines? Both are large graphics apps (~1GB each) so probably personal-only. (2) Confirm alphabetical positions: 'gimp' between 'ghostty' and 'google-chrome'; 'inkscape' immediately before 'insomnia'.
notes: Backlog — user asked to park this for later during the 2026-04-11 session (after the fast-CI work landed). No urgency.
close_reason: Delivered in PR pmgledhill102/dotfiles#129. All 7 CI checks green in ~30s. Awaiting human review and merge. Post-merge full test-install on main will exercise the actual brew bundle install of both casks — first real-world download test of ~2GB combined.

title: Print list of installed dot* shell functions at end of dotup / dotinstall
description: After dotup (and the initial install flow) finishes, print a summary of the custom shell functions that have been installed, each with a one-line description. Serves as a reminder of what commands are available and what they do. Should work across multiple function files in home/dot_config/zsh/functions/ and auto-update as new function files are added.
design: Add a new shell function (probably 'dotfuncs' or reuse dotstatus) that iterates home/.config/zsh/functions/*.zsh, reads the first comment line after the shebang+shellcheck pragma as the function description, and prints each as 'funcname - description'. Call this helper at the end of dotup(). For the install flow (investigate what 'dotinstall' actually maps to — likely install.sh or a run_once chezmoi script), either source the functions dir and call the helper, or print a static equivalent. Function description comments should be standardised across files for consistent output.
acceptance_criteria: (1) dotup prints 'Custom functions available:' followed by a list of functions and descriptions at the end of a successful run. (2) Same list appears at the end of the install flow. (3) Adding a new function file under home/dot_config/zsh/functions/ automatically shows up in the list on next dotup. (4) shellcheck clean. (5) Works on macOS (and Linux if install flow supports it).
notes: Depends on task dotfiles-pcg having been merged (it has). Scope: may touch dotup.zsh, a new dotfuncs.zsh (or similar), the existing function files to standardise header comments, and possibly install.sh.
close_reason: Delivered as PRs #125 (note/notes CLI ergonomics) and #126 (dotfuncs listing). Both all 7 CI checks green. Independent branches, both merge cleanly from main. Awaiting human review and merge per project policy.

title: Allow Bash(command -v *) to match existing Bash(which *)
description: The POSIX `command -v` synonym of `which` was missing from the Claude Code allowedTools list. Every tool-availability chain like `command -v shellcheck && shellcheck --version` required manual approval even though the target tool (shellcheck, etc.) was individually permitted — the permission matcher checks each sub-command in a compound command separately, so a missing leading match blocks the whole thing. Discovered during the note function session (dotfiles-jvb).
notes: Retroactively created after the work was already committed and pushed — should have been created before editing settings.json per the new 'always create a beads issue' rule in home/dot_claude/CLAUDE.md.tmpl. Filing here for audit-trail completeness. Delivered as a follow-up commit on the same branch as dotfiles-jvb (PR #123), bundled at user request.
close_reason: Delivered in commit 60f273c on PR pmgledhill102/dotfiles#123. settings.json + settings.json.md updated. CI green. Retroactive issue — work already complete when created.

title: Evaluate Podman over Docker for container tooling
description: Investigate replacing Docker Desktop with Podman. Currently podman is in the personal brew formulas list. Need to evaluate whether Docker Desktop (not currently in the dotfiles) should be dropped in favor of Podman-only, or if both are needed.
close_reason: Evaluated and implemented: Podman chosen over Docker for all environments.

title: Investigate wider fzf + zoxide integrations
description: Now that fzf and zoxide are wired in minimally, evaluate which optional integrations are worth adopting. Try one at a time, keep what proves useful day-to-day, drop the rest. Candidates: zoxide --cmd cd (replace cd entirely); fzf-tab (fzf-driven tab completion); forgit (fzf-driven git add/log/diff); FZF_DEFAULT_COMMAND using fd; FZF_CTRL_T_OPTS preview pane with bat; lazygit fzf integration; FZF_DEFAULT_OPTS theming.
